meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Fix Feature Creep: User Onboarding for SaaS Founders
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement high-converting user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive revenue and user retention today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of feedback loops. By implementing structured user onboarding, you force yourself to define the 'Aha!' moment early. Use Antigravity to scaffold your flow and Claude Code to iterate. This turns a sprawling feature list into a streamlined, revenue-generating MVP.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' products that never saw the light of day. You start with a vision of a comprehensive platform, but six months later, you're drowning in technical debt, staring at a half-finished dashboard that does everything and nothing at all. The culprit isn't your coding ability; it's the lack of a defined path to value.

User onboarding is the tactical cure for this paralysis. By forcing yourself to build the bridge between the user's first click and their first success, you strip away the bloat. We aren't just building a tour; we are building a conversion engine that proves your business model before you waste another month on unused features.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Milestone
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your product delivers value. Everything else is secondary.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current feature set and identify the single 'Aha!' moment that provides the most value to a new user. Generate a sequence of 3 steps to guide them there.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify the core value milestone from codebase and outline a 3-step onboarding flow'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding Context
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code for an onboarding state machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React state machine for a 3-step onboarding process. Include persistent storage to track user progress in the database.

steps.title: Implement Just-In-Time Tooltips
steps.description: Don't overwhelm users. Only show help when they reach the specific feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable Tooltip component that triggers based on the user's onboarding progress state.

steps.title: Build Automated Progress Tracking
steps.description: Sync onboarding completion with your backend to trigger post-onboarding emails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a backend service that updates the user_onboarding_status in Supabase whenever a step is completed.

steps.title: Validate with Analytics Hooks
steps.description: Monitor drop-off points to iterate on your flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add PostHog tracking events to each step of the onboarding flow to identify where users quit.

pro_tips: Never build an onboarding tour that takes more than 60 seconds; if it takes longer, your product is too complex.
Use 'empty states' as an onboarding tool—if a user hasn't created data, show them a CTA to create it, not a blank screen.
Always A/B test your onboarding CTA button text; 'Get Started' is rarely as effective as 'Launch My Project'.
Track the time-to-first-value (TTFV) as your primary KPI; if it increases, your onboarding is broken.
Give users a 'skip' option; forcing users through onboarding creates resentment, not retention.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment and watch one user navigate it in real-time using a tool like FullStory.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for your customers. By focusing on onboarding, you eliminate feature creep and sharpen your focus on what actually drives revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
